steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Slope-intercept form is $f(x)=mx+b$ where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept ($f(0)$).

steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: Given: slope $m=\frac{5}{6}$ and $f(0)=4$, so $b=4$. The function is $f(x)=\frac{5}{6}x+4$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content:
Evaluate choices:
  • A ($f(x)=\frac{5}{6}x-4$): correct slope, wrong intercept ($-4$ instead of $4$) — eliminated.
  • B ($f(x)=\frac{5}{6}x+4$): slope $\frac{5}{6}$ and $f(0)=4$. B is correct.
  • C ($f(x)=6x-4$): uses the denominator of the slope as the slope and has a negative intercept — eliminated.
  • D ($f(x)=6x+4$): uses the denominator of the slope as the slope, not $\frac{5}{6}$ — eliminated.
